## 🔬 Top Findings

### 1. Anthropic's Agent Marketplace Experiment (Project Deal)
Anthropic ran a classified marketplace where AI agents represented buyers and sellers negotiating real deals with real money. 69 employees participated with $100 budgets. **Key insight**: when users were represented by more advanced models, they got "objectively better outcomes" — a stark preview of what agent-to-agent commerce means for consumer welfare and market power. The initial instructions given to agents had no effect on sale likelihood or prices — the model capability dominated.

### 2. Amazon + OpenAI Deepen Partnership; OpenAI Ends Exclusive Microsoft Deal
OpenAI restructured its Microsoft partnership — OpenAI can now expand with AWS and Google Cloud freely. Codex is now available (limited preview) via Amazon Bedrock alongside OpenAI's latest models. This shifts the competitive landscape: OpenAI is no longer bound to Microsoft, and enterprises with existing AWS commitments can now adopt Codex without Azure migration. The $100/month Codex Pro tier was also noted.

### 3. Claude's Creative Tool Expansion: From Coding to Full Production Pipelines
Anthropic released 9 connectors for creative tools including Blender, Adobe, Ableton, Autodesk Fusion, SketchUp. Claude can now work across the full animation/design pipeline — not just assist with writing, but act as a creative collaborator within professional creative software. This positions Claude as a production tool, not just an assistant. Claude Design product also launched.

---

## 💡 Key Insights

### 🔐 Security — Agentic AI Attack Surfaces Are Real
CISA + agencies from UK and Australia published a joint report warning that every component in an agentic AI system widens the attack surface. Autonomous agents are easy to misuse and breach — productivity losses and private data compromise are concrete risks, not theoretical. **Defense-in-depth is recommended.** This is a direct challenge to the "move fast with AI agents" camp.

### 💸 Payments — AI Agents Can Now Pay Real Merchants
Clink launched the world's first fiat agentic payment skill — AI agents can now make real purchases on behalf of users using existing credit cards, under user-defined limits. Two early merchants (ModelMax, PollyReach) are live. This marks the beginning of **agent-to-merchant commerce at scale** and is likely to accelerate regulatory attention on agentic transactions.

### 🏭 Manufacturing — AI Agents as the Missing Link in Factory Automation
Analysis from robotics/automation sector: AI agents can detect defect rate changes, trace to supplier issues, draft purchase orders, notify managers, and adjust production schedules — all in under a minute without explicit programming for the scenario. This is qualitatively different from traditional automation and is driving serious industrial adoption conversations.

### 📊 Market — CPU Inference is Back
Intel's Q1 2026 earnings revealed $5.1B DCAI revenue (+22% YoY), with CEO highlighting a shift from GPU-only setups toward **CPU-centric inference and agentic AI workloads**. ARM surged 15%. The implication: as inference workloads grow and agents become multi-step, the CPU's role in orchestration and light inference is being revalued. GPU dominance is not inevitable for all AI workloads.

---

## ⚡ Practical Takeaways

1. **Start monitoring agentic AI security posture.** With CISA/UK/AU issuing joint warnings, enterprise security teams will need AI agent governance frameworks in 2026. If you're deploying agents, get ahead of this — document agent permissions, monitor for drift, and implement layered defenses.

2. **Codex on AWS Bedrock lowers enterprise adoption friction.** If your org has AWS commitments and existing compliance workflows, the new Codex + Bedrock integration means you can deploy AI coding agents without migrating to Azure. Worth re-evaluating your AI coding agent strategy if you've been waiting for cloud-provider flexibility.

3. **Local LLM for bug-finding is production-ready.** Greg Kroah-Hartman approved the Linux kernel "Clanker" bot running on AMD Ryzen AI Max + Framework Laptop — fully offline, finding real kernel bugs. If you have hardware with dedicated NPUs (Intel Lunar Lake, Apple Silicon, AMD Strix), local agentic workflows for code review/security scanning are viable today.
